threat_model: GitHub action execution can mutate repository state when an installation token is used for check-run writes or branch-protection reconciliation. The main risks are unapproved execution, raw token or JWT disclosure, repository mismatch between token plan and action plan, forged response evidence, accepting non-2xx responses as executed, overclaiming dry-run proof as live execution, and leaking private-key or token material through payload or metadata. The mitigation is to keep the planner non-live, hash the action payload, require token plan repository identity to match the action repository, block secret-shaped material, require readiness evidence refs for all modes, forbid execution evidence in plan-only and dry-run modes, and require operator approval plus token-exchange receipt, external execution receipt, 2xx status, and response hash before execute-approved receipts admit an action execution claim.

required_checks.check: plan-only and dry-run check proving the planner performs no GitHub API call, authenticates no request, stores no raw token, and rejects execution response evidence
receipt integrity check proving execute-approved mode requires token-exchange and external execution receipts before admission
audit visibility check proving repository, token repository, action kind, endpoint, method, and payload are bound into deterministic receipt evidence
redaction and secret absence check proving raw token-shaped metadata blocks receipt admission
policy bypass check proving non-execute modes reject approval, token-exchange, external execution, response status, and response hash evidence
IDOR tenant-scope check proving tenant id, actor id, command id, action repository, and token repository are carried and repository mismatch blocks execution admission
auth boundary check proving execute-approved mode requires approval and token-exchange receipt evidence before external execution admission
protocol manifest check proving the public GitHub action execution receipt schema is indexed

mitigations: require readiness evidence refs for all action execution receipts
forbid execution response evidence in plan-only and dry-run modes
require token plan repository identity to match action repository identity
require approval, token-exchange receipt, external execution receipt, 2xx response, and response payload hash for execute-approved mode
block raw token, JWT, and private-key shaped material before receipt admission
index the public receipt schema in the governance protocol manifest
avoid request authentication, GitHub API calls, workflow dispatch, branch protection mutation, check-run mutation, deployment, or production claims
